name: BigCommerce
one_liner: SaaS ecommerce platform for building online stores, with a comprehensive REST/GraphQL Admin API for managing catalog, orders, customers, and storefront.
primary_auth: OAuth2 access token passed via the X-Auth-Token header (store-level or app-level API accounts)
access_notes: Free developer sandbox stores are available via the BigCommerce Partner Portal (self-serve partner registration with a company email). Store owners create API accounts under Settings > API Accounts to instantly generate OAuth credentials (client id/secret + access token) with selected scopes. No payment or human approval needed for a working sandbox and credentials; sandboxes cannot process live transactions.
api_notes: Extensive Admin REST API (v2 and v3) covering catalog/products, orders, customers, carts, checkouts, pricing, promotions, shipping, payments, settings, etc., plus a GraphQL Admin API and a GraphQL Storefront API. Well over 100 operations.
mcp_notes: BigCommerce (the vendor) ships an official Storefront MCP server enabling AI agents to search catalog, manage carts, and generate checkout URLs; enabled by store owners via Settings > Early Access. Multiple community/third-party MCP servers (isaacgounton, Zapier, StackOne, CData, Pipedream) also exist.
main_blocker: None significant: public API with self-serve OAuth credentials from a free sandbox/store control panel. Partner registration is required to create a free sandbox but is self-serve.

evidence.claim: Most REST and GraphQL Admin endpoints authenticate via the X-Auth-Token header using OAuth-based store/app/account-level API accounts.
Store API accounts are created in the control panel (Settings > API Accounts) with configurable OAuth scopes.
Free sandbox stores for development/testing are created through the Partner Portal after self-serve partner registration.
BigCommerce ships an official Storefront MCP server for AI shopping agents, enabled via Settings > Early Access.

open_questions: Whether partner registration for a free sandbox ever requires manual review/approval in practice (docs describe it as self-serve); exact scope coverage of the official Storefront MCP vs. the full Admin API.
